reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD and .install script as safe. The PKGBUILD uses verified HTTPS sources with checksums, and the install script only contains informational messages. No code executes during critical phases beyond standard firmware extraction.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped analysis of broadcom-wl-*.tar.bz2 binary firmware blob. While difficult to analyze, firmware files could theoretically contain malicious code that executes when loaded by kernel drivers.
false_negatives: Potential firmware blob risks (coverage gap rather than model error)
SHA1 checksum use (cryptographically weak) noted by qwen but not flagged as risk - though not strictly malicious
learnings: Improve binary file handling: Consider basic firmware analysis (file headers, known paths) rather than full skip
Prioritize checksum strength assessment in PKGBUILDs
Clarify binary file policy: Document this blind spot for human reviewers
Both models correctly focused on build/install hooks - validate this file prioritization logic
